rows.text:
Not everyone online is who they say they are. 🚨

Kendall Carter is serving 30 years in federal prison for victimizing more than a dozen teens and children.

His case from @DHSgov’s national public awareness campaign, @Know2Protect, is a powerful reminder: Offenders often use fake profiles to manipulate and exploit kids online.

rows.text:
EXCLUSIVE VIDEO: Disabling fire needed to stop non-compliant vessel with 64 Illegal Aliens onboard!
 
On May 9, our Michel O. Maceda Marine Unit detected and interdicted a non-compliant Yola-type vessel carrying 64 individuals on the western side of Puerto Rico.
 
Marine Interdiction Agents discovered 64 illegal aliens from the Dominican Republic and Haiti onboard. The subjects were transferred onto AMO and PRPD FURA marine assets and transported to the Mayaguez Port of Entry. Border Patrol agents, with support from HSI and PRPD, processed the group at the Ramey Border Patrol Station for removal proceedings. #BorderSecurity
rows.text_resolved:

rows.text:
An alien who is in the U.S. temporarily and wants a Green Card must return to their home country to apply.

This policy allows our immigration system to function as the law intended instead of incentivizing loopholes.

The era of abusing our nation’s immigration system is over.

rows.text:
Five defendants pleaded guilty to federal firearms offenses while a federal grand jury indicted eight others in an international firearms trafficking conspiracy that funneled illegally obtained weapons from New Hampshire through the Akwesasne Mohawk Indian Reservation in New York.

ICE @HSIBuffalo, NY, and @ATFHQ led the investigation that identified about 50 firearms potentially trafficked from New Hampshire to Canada through Vermont and New York, several of which were recovered at various crime scenes, to include kidnapping and attempted murder, in Canada.

rows.text:
ICE’s first interaction with Alejandro Paulino-Espidio was in 2006, when he was found among 17 illegal aliens hiding in the back of a van. He was granted voluntary return to Mexico, but that wasn’t the last ICE would see of him.

He snuck back into the United States by climbing a border fence in 2018. When Border Patrol agents found him, he claimed he planned to live with some friends in Orange County, California. Federal officials deported him that time.

But he returned illegally — again. 

Then, in 2022, a California judge in Fresno County convicted him of arson. A few years later, a California judge in Merced County convicted him of escaping from custody and vandalism.

➡️ICE deported Paulino-Espidio May 16. His repeated disregard of Americans boundaries, laws and safety shows clearly why @DHSgov has prioritized immigration enforcement and removal AND border security.
rows.text_resolved:

rows.text:
SCOOP: An illegal immigrant released into the country under the Biden admin was accused of raping a 12-year-old girl in Missouri, resulting in her pregnancy.

Investigators said Colombian national Brayanne Escobar-Guarnizo even admitted to a detective that “we only had sex one time” and acknowledged possessing nude images of the young victim on his phone.

Authorities arrested Escobar-Guarnizo in Greene County, Missouri, in 2024 after the child’s mother contacted law enforcement upon discovering her daughter was pregnant.

https://t.co/2CSPhJ7fPW

rows.text:
ICE @EROBuffalo arrested suspected MS-13 gang member and Salvadoran illegal alien Josue Coreas-Chavez when he was released from Wyoming Correctional Facility in Attica, NY.  

He was sentenced for burglary, conspiracy and criminal facilitation. He’s also got pending charges for robbery, kidnapping, assault, coercion, unlawful imprisonment and grand larceny.

He’ll remain in custody pending a deportation decision.

2057900574268375128.description: Vertical (9:16) video with a DHS/Know2Protect logo, blue-black color grade, and grainy thermal-style B-roll. All-caps title cards read 'WANT TO KNOW WHY?' and 'PROTECT YOURSELF'. One scene shows a teen hunched face-down on a bed with floating chat bubbles ('Promise I wont leak. Just 1 more', 'WYD?', 'You up?'). A narrator speaks about a person named Kendall Carter, stating he is serving 30 years in federal prison, over an instrumental music bed.
2057900574268375128.summary_text: Vertical (9:16) video with a DHS/Know2Protect logo, blue-black color grade, and grainy thermal-style B-roll. All-caps title cards read 'WANT TO KNOW WHY?' and 'PROTECT YOURSELF'. One scene shows a teen hunched face-down on a bed with floating chat bubbles ('Promise I wont leak. Just 1 more', 'WYD?', 'You up?'). A narrator speaks about a person named Kendall Carter, stating he is serving 30 years in federal prison, over an instrumental music bed.  [0:06] Near-black gradient with caption 'WANT TO KNOW WHY?' (KNOW in orange); instrumental music; narrator: 'Meet Kendall Carter. Kendall is spending 30 years in federal prison. Want to know why?'  [0:18] Grainy high-contrast thermal/black-and-white abstract B-roll (haze over a bright bar of light); narrator: 'For over a year, he used multiple fake online profiles of teen boys and girls to meet kids. He groomed them.'  [0:30] A teen is hunched face-down on a bed, head in arms, with floating chat bubbles above ('Girl, you a baddie. U look so good', 'Promise I wont leak. Just 1 more', 'Everyone is gonna know', 'You ghosting me??', 'WYD?', 'You up?'); narrator states he tricked kids into sending images then threatened to leak them.  [0:43] Dark cutaway footage; narrator: 'There were multiple victims, one as young as 12. This is called sextortion, and it's a crime,' and states Carter's devices held 100+ explicit images of minors. On-screen text states the 100+ figure.  [0:55] Caption 'PROTECT YOURSELF' over the dark gradient; narrator: 'Nearly half of all kids have been approached online... Not everyone you meet online is who they say they are. Protect yourself online. Learn how at know2protect.gov.'

2057810020117495838.description: Vertical infographic on a black background. The top half has an all-caps white headline in a heavy sans-serif font reading '13 CHARGED IN ICE HSI & ATF-LED PROBE INTO GUN SMUGGLING RING EXPLOITING THE U.S.-CANADA BORDER', with the words 'CHARGED IN' and 'EXPLOITING THE' enlarged. Centered is an inset aerial or satellite map (green and orange shaded regions) with red labeled pins and a red route line marking 'Canadian Border Crossing', 'Akwesasne (Ontario)', 'Akwesasne (New York state)', and 'US Border Crossing'. The bottom portion shows photographs of multiple black semi-automatic handguns laid out, including a gloved hand holding one firearm at lower right. A large faint ghosted 'ICE' watermark spans the lower area, alongside the DHS Homeland Security seal and a small ATF/HSI seal next to bold 'HSI' text.

2057628813941473741.summary_text: photo; 314x285; 0.19 MiB; archived; tweet context: ICE’s first interaction with Alejandro Paulino-Espidio was in 2006, when he was found among 17 illegal aliens hiding in the back of a van. He was granted voluntary return to Mexico, but that wasn’t the last ICE would see of him. He snuck back into the United...; source URL: https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HI4qgKlXAAAKbmQ.png; archive URL: https://github.com/vidproject/x/releases/download/media-ICEgov/3_2057628811840126976.png; needs OCR, transcript, or frame-level vision before content claims. ICE’s first interaction with Alejandro Paulino-Espidio was in 2006, when he was found among 17 illegal aliens hiding in the back of a van. He was granted voluntary return to Mexico, but that wasn’t the last ICE would see of him. He snuck back into the United States by climbing a border fence in 2018. When Border Patrol agents found him, he claimed he planned to live with some friends in Orange County, California. Federal officials deported him that time. But he returned illegally — again. Then, in 2022, a California judge in Fresno County convicted him of arson. A few years later, a California judge in Merced County convicted him of escaping from custody and vandalism. ➡️ICE deported Paulino-Espidio May 16. His repeated disregard of Americans boundaries, laws and safety shows clearly why @DHSgov has prioritized immigration enforcement and removal AND border security.

2057608687254946152.description: Memorial 'Wall of Honor' card from ICE. The background is a blurred color photograph of two deep-red roses lying on a dark slate surface with an out-of-focus American flag draped behind at upper right. Inset at the upper left, on a black field, is a gold-and-blue badge reading 'U.S. CUSTOMS - THE DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY' at top and 'SPECIAL AGENT' on the bottom banner, with a central Treasury seal dated 1789. Across the bottom, white serif typography reads 'Wall of Honor' (large) beside an ICE blue-ribbon pin, with the text: 'Special Agent John M. Lacey', a gold-ruled divider, 'End of Watch', and the date 'Saturday, May 21, 1977'. The tweet text states Special Agent John M. Lacey died in 1977 from a New Jersey auto-accident injury.
